picks.analysis: The White Sox have an Over record of 8-2-0 in their last 10 games.
The White Sox have won five of seven against the Tigets, with four of those wins coming by at least two runs.

Will Warren's optimized release slot helps his 3,005-RPM sweeper generate elite horizontal movement, making it a tough matchup for Oakland's lineup. With New York's bullpen leading MLB in rolling groundball rate, play Yankees -1.5 to -105.

The Toronto Blue Jays and Baltimore Orioles will finish their series on Sunday afternoon. They are the Peacock game, so be aware of the early start time. The Blue Jays won the first 2 games in this series, but the O’s bounced back on Saturday afternoon with an improbable 9th-inning rally, scoring 5 runs in the final frame to win the game 6-5. Baltimore will look to carry that momentum into Sunday’s series finale, when they will have their ace on the mound. I like how this game sets up for the Orioles, and I am taking them to win on Sunday. Kyle Bradish has been pitching well in May, turning in 4 excellent starts in a row — and against good offenses. In fact, he logged quality starts against the Rays, Yankees and Athletics in that stretch. On the other side, the Blue Jays will go with Spencer Miles, a guy who has spent most of the season in the bullpen but has been forced into the rotation because of all the injuries to Toronto’s staff. Miles has put up good ratios, but he isn’t stretched out, so this becomes somewhat of a bullpen game. I like the way the Baltimore offense is starting to take shape now that most of their hitters are healthy. Give me the orange birds on Sunday. Blue Jays vs Orioles Prediction : Baltimore Orioles ML (-130) available at the time of publishing. Playable to -140 . The Minnesota Twins and the Pittsburgh Pirates have given fans a great series at PNC Park, with the home team winning 6-5 and 10-9 across the first 2 game. Those contests included plenty of offensive outbursts, but the recent play of Zebby Matthews and Braxton Ashcroft could provide us with an intriguing pitching matchup in the final game of the series. Matthews has just 3 starts this season, but he looks to be on the way to his best season in his 3-year MLB career — going 6+ innings in each of the appearances and owning a 2.37 ERA. The righty’s underlying numbers are great, as he doesn’t give up a ton of hard contact, and he has great control. It’s a perfect mix for him to put the Twins in a position to take at least 1 of the 3 games from the Pirates and avoid the sweep. Matthews isn’t the only starter in this game with great underlying numbers. Ashcraft’s 2.94 xERA proves that his success this season is no fluke and that the team’s choice to transition him into the rotation full-time was a great decision. The righty allowed just 1 earned run in 6+ innings in each of his past 2 starts. We’ll take the pitching matchup in this game and back the bats to go cold. Twins vs Pirates prediction: Under 7.5 (-105) available at time of publishing. Playable to that number. It isn’t exactly shaping up to be a pitcher’s duel on Sunday afternoon when the San Diego Padres visit the Washington Nationals for their final game of the series, but that isn’t a massive difference from the rest of the series, as the teams combined for 25 runs over the first 2 games. Griffin Canning didn’t make his season debut until May after missing the first month and a half on the injured list, and he immediately made an impact with a solid 5-inning outing. However, that has been his only great performance of the season thus far, allowing 6 runs in back-to-back games after his first start, and then 3 runs in back-to-back games heading into this matchup. Despite the inflated 7.54 ERA, he hasn’t been quite as bad as those numbers suggest, owning a 4.58 xERA. Zack Littell had a rough stretch through April, but the righty has seen his ERA drop considerably in May, allowing just 6 earned runs through 24.2 innings. While Canning’s xERA paints him in a better light, Littell’s suggests that there could be a regression to the mean coming after escaping danger in May, as he owns a 6.57 xERA compared to his 5.23 ERA. The starter’s xERA puts him in the bottom 3% of the entire MLB. This starting pitcher matchup is a bit scary, with both showing some inconsistencies. However, the underlying numbers and the bullpen advantage for San Diego makes me lean the Padres in this one. Padres vs Nationals prediction: San Diego Padres ML (-130) available at time of publishing. Playable to that number. The Boston Red Sox offense finally came to life Saturday, erupting for 9 runs on 11 hits in a convincing win over the Cleveland Guardians. Cleveland struggled to generate offense, managing just 1 run on 6 hits, while committing 4 costly errors. Looking to secure a series win, the Red Sox will hand the ball over to the lefty, Ranger Suarez, who enters with a 2-3 record and a 3.02 ERA. For the Guardians, Tanner Bibee will get the nod, who is winless — holding a 0-7 record with a 4.57 ERA. Boston has been the hotter team offensively over the last 10 games, averaging 5.2 runs per game while batting an impressive .293. As for Cleveland, they’re averaging 2.3 runs per game and hitting .227 in that span. Taking a look at the starting pitchers, Bibee is coming off one of his worst performances of the season against the Nationals, when he gave up 7 runs on 8 hits in 3 innings pitched. However, Suarez wasn’t much better in his last start, giving up 5 runs on 6 hits in 5 innings against the Braves. Taking everything into consideration, the Red Sox offense has shown it is more than capable of producing runs when it matters the most. Given Bibee’s struggles this season, I’m going to lean towards the Red Sox to secure a series win on the road behind Ranger Suarez. Red Sox vs Guardians prediction: Boston Red Sox ML (-115) at the time of publishing. Playable to – 130. You can join Polymarket by clicking here and trade on our Red Sox vs Guardians predictions.

The Los Angeles Angels and Tampa Bay Rays wrap up a three-game series at Tropicana Field in St. Petersburg, and it’s a rubber game. The Rays picked up an 8-5 win as moderate favorites (-162) on Friday as the Over (8) cashed, while the Halos trounced Tampa 14-3 as moderate underdogs (+144) as the Over (7) easily connected. It was a rare loss for the Rays at home, as they’re 20-6 under the Tropicana roof. It was equally a rare win for the Angels on the road, as they’re just 11-20 away from the Big A. The Angels turn to RHP Jack Kochanowicz (2-3, 4.99 ERA, 1.39 WHIP) for Sunday’s start. He is a 1-1 with a dismal 6.25 ERA in 31 2/3 IP across six road starts, and his biggest issue has been issuing walks. He has 18 walks in six starts away from home. The Rays counter with RHP Shane McClanahan (5-2, 2.52 ERA, 1.04 WHIP), who has exhibited All-Star form through 10 starts and 50 IP. The secret to his success has been keeping the ball in the yard, as he has allowed just two homers. He has had a slight problem with walks, allowing two or more walks in six of his 10 outings, but he has managed to limit the damage. At home, McClanahan is a perfect 4-0 with a 2.10 ERA across 25 2/3 IP in five starts, and teams are hitting just .185 against him there. Rays -1.5 (+115) at time of publishing. Playable to -110.

The New York Yankees and Athletics play the series finale on Sunday, and it’s a rubber game. The Yankees routed the A’s 8-2 on Friday as moderate favorites on Friday behind LHP Carlos Rodon, while the A’s picked up a 6-4 victory on Saturday as moderate underdogs (+130). The Over cashed both games, just in case you’re considering a same-game parlay (SGP). However, let’s focus on the side for our purposes. The Yankees are 18-14 on the road this season, while the Athletics are just 11-16 in their temporary home in West Sacramento. The Yankees roll with RHP Will Warren (6-1, 3.55 ERA, 1.17 WHIP) for Sunday’s game, and he has actually been much better away from home. Warren is 2-0 with a 2.86 ERA while allowing just a single homer across 22 IP in four starts, and teams are hitting just .229 against him this season on the road. The A’s counter with LHP Jacob Lopez (4-2, 5.73 ERA, 1.77 WHIP). He is 0-1 with a 5.95 ERA across 19 2/3 IP in four home starts, serving up five homers with 13 walks in the span. As such, we’re going with the Yankees to get the job done, and to do so by at least two runs. Yankees -1.5 (+105) at time of publishing. Playable to -110.
